item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The note at the end just… burst the tone for me. Was a simple, uncommon and effective item that left room for the imagination. Then suddendly 'and it can trap you! Spooky!'

Sorry, but -1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My one request is that the kanji in the description have a footnote with a translation. Other than that, I'm not too sure about what else to change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was pretty nice but had some issues. Minor formatting ones included the blackboxing of the number of pages of sheet music and the time of unconsciousness - not sure why that would need to be blackboxed - and the first Japanese text (樺岡) should probably have a translation, since all the song names did, although I know it got explained later.

I agree with PeppersGhost that the compulsion effect is unnecessary and I also disliked the note, which I thought was tonally jarring. A more nebulous criticism of mine is that this feels like something which ought to have more meaning and backstory than it does. Reading through this, I want to find some sort of reasoning or internal logic behind this, but it doesn't seem to be there unless I'm being obtuse. I'm not saying it needs lots of extra details, since its simplicity and brevity is part of the charm, but I would have preferred some more hints to what is going on, or at least an indication that there is something more going on here. The note at the end is also a problem with this as the 'twist' (that people get trapped in the historical world) just comes out of nowhere - it doesn't tie into any information presented before.

I novoted, as the charm of the piece mitigated my complaints.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: True, but the piece is nice either way…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A nice little piece, but I feel like that last note completely takes away most of its charm. It just doesn't feel necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As someone who knows nothing about japanese culture, I understood this just fine. Anything you need to know you can gather from context, anything above that you can gather from Google.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the simple, uncomplicated effect of this SCP. It's a refreshing, straightforward, item that doesn't involve world ending shenanigans, and I am perfectly ok with that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It comes off as a fairly standard sort of anomalous effect, though I will say the interview giving us a time and place was kind of interesting. This really needs some culture context notes, though. It's almost incomprehensible at times unless the reader is highly familiar with Japanese culture. Novote.

Researcher ███████ is native in Japanese.

This should be "is fluent in Japanese" or "is a native speaker of Japanese".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What a lovely little piece. There's a sweetness to it that really appeals to me. The note at the end caught me off guard, but it's an effective contrast to the rest of the article. I could have done without the compulsion effect, since it doesn't really add anything and feels a bit trite in an otherwise solid piece, but it's a small quibble. +1
